title: Higher-Order Functions
description: Implement your own versions of JavaScript's built-in higher-order array methods: map, filter, and reduce. This exercise deepens your understanding of how these fundamental functions work under the hood.
instructions: Create a function named `myMap` that mimics Array.prototype.map()
Create a function named `myFilter` that mimics Array.prototype.filter()
Create a function named `myReduce` that mimics Array.prototype.reduce()
Each function should accept an array and appropriate callback/parameters
Implement the logic without using the built-in array methods
learningObjectives: Understand how higher-order functions work internally
Learn to pass functions as arguments

testCases.name: myReduce sums array with initial value

hints.content: For myMap and myFilter, create a result array and iterate through the input array with a for loop. Call the callback function for each element.
For myReduce, use an accumulator variable. If initialValue is provided, start with that; otherwise use the first array element and start iterating from index 1.
Remember to pass all parameters to callbacks: myMap/myFilter callbacks receive (element, index, array), and myReduce callback receives (accumulator, element, index, array).
